creator: drosih@rpi.edu
text:
The patch given with this PR isn't quite right.  It wrecks things
for scanning in sub-directories of /usr/ports.  The idea is that a
'make search' in /usr/ports will search all of ports, but the same
'make search' while in /usr/ports/shells (for instance) will only
search thru the shell-related ports for the name or key in question.

I have an alternate patch to fix a different problem in:
     http://www.FreeBSD.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/31862

(ignore the patch in the initial PR, and instead use the one in my
followup to it)

In limited testing, I *think* it also solves this problem.  When
testing this, though, make sure you have done a 'make index' while
in your alternate PORTSDIR.  If you created that alternate ports
directory by just 'cvsup'-ing to the alternate location, then all
the entries in ${PORTSDIR}/INDEX will still point to the standard
location (ie, /usr/ports).

I guess this implies that perhaps the INDEX should be formatted
a bit differently, with maybe some header line giving what *it*
thinks the ports directory is, and then all the entries giving a
relative location from that ports directory.  I am not ambitious
enough to figure out all the ramifications of that idea, though.

-- 
Garance Alistair Drosehn            =   gad@eclipse.acs.rpi.edu
Senior Systems Programmer           or  gad@freebsd.org
Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute    or  drosih@rpi.edu
